scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, re-confirmed against the live docs page (https://xkcd.com/json.html, fetched 2026-07-03) and the live JSON responses (https://xkcd.com/info.0.json, https://xkcd.com/614/info.0.json): xkcd's documented JSON interface publishes EXACTLY two endpoint shapes, both already implemented as streams below. There is no list/search/index JSON endpoint (the JSON API has no way to enumerate or query comics; a caller wanting comic #N must already know N), no other GET resource, and no mutation capability of any kind (xkcd is a static, unauthenticated, publicly-cached comic site with no accounts, no user data, and no documented write path) — this is not a scoping choice, it is the full extent of the real surface. Non-JSON site surfaces (the HTML site itself, /atom.xml, /rss.xml, the What-If blog, the third-party explainxkcd.com wiki) are separate products from a separate/undocumented interface and are out of scope for this JSON-API connector, not omissions from it.
